figures.caption: Table 1: Comparison between GEODRIVE-BENCH and existing autonomous driving benchmarks/datasets. Modality: S.I. = single image, M.I. = multi-image, M.V. = multi-view. Tasks: Perception, pRediction, pLanning, rEgion understanding, Spatial/numerical, Corner cases, Openloop planning. Rule denotes explicit grounding in local traffic regulations; Culture denotes reasoning about region-specific driving conventions. # QA reports the size of the test set.
Table 3: Cross-country standard deviation of overall accuracy (in %) on GEODRIVE-BENCH. For each (setting, model) pair, we report the standard deviation across six countries.

Table 8: Construction funnel from raw QA candidate to released benchmark. Verdict distribution rows correspond to the language-model judge audit; the final retained set additionally incorporates human review of borderline cases through the annotation tool.
Figure 8: Web-based annotation tool used for human review.

Figure 4: Ablation on traffic rule injection across the four task categories. Rule-Given provides the correct rule, Wrong-Rule injects a mismatched rule, and Full-Handbook provides the full multicountry handbook.

Table 6: Robustness to image perturbation on GEODRIVE-BENCH (5,053 items). For each base VLM we report overall accuracy (%) under three input conditions (Normal: original image; No Image: image removed; Image Corruption: Gaussian blur at ImageNet-C severity 3) crossed with three prompting strategies (Direct, Reasoning, Rule-Given). Within each (model, prompt) column triplet we bold the highest accuracy.

Figure 5: Country-wise distribution of error types for InternVL3 and our DRIVEOPD ‡ under the reasoning setting. Each pie chart shows the proportion of four major error categories within a country: Visual Misperception, Geographic Misclassification, Cultural Rule Gap, and Reasoning Error.
Figure 6: Illustration of the reasoning processes of InternVL3 for school-warning sign questions .

Table 7: Rule-context ablation per task category on GEODRIVE-BENCH (5,053 items). Each cell reports overall accuracy (%) for the given (task category, prompt setting, model) triplet. RuleGiven provides the cited country-specific rule; Wrong-Rule replaces it with a deterministically sampled non-applicable rule from the same country; Full-Handbook substitutes the cited snippet with the entire country handbook. DRIVEOPD † and DRIVEOPD ‡ are our distilled checkpoints over Qwen2.5-VL-7B and InternVL3-8B respectively. Companion to Fig. 4 in the main paper.

Figure 7: Country-wise distribution of error types for Qwen2.5-VL-7B and DRIVEOPD † under the Reasoning setting. Each pie chart shows the proportion of four error categories within a country: Visual Misperception, Geographic Misclassification, Cultural Rule Gap, and Reasoning Error.
Figure 3: Distribution of GEODRIVE-BENCH across datasets, task categories, countries, scenarios, and region-specific topics.
Table 5: DRIVEOPD training hyperparameters, shared by DRIVEOPD † (Qwen2.5-VL-7B) and DRIVEOPD ‡ (InternVL3-8B).
